Unable to pay bail; financial constraint danger

safety_signals.meaning: Safe location to store coins; refuge from financial loss

Safe zone for resource collection if entry requirements met

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player sprite contrasted against police authority figures
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Holli's apartment building, Slash Club, Las Vegas, malt shop—civilian recreational/illicit spaces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Minimal police presence; open access to most locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Popper Police avoid or ignore player; smooth movement through world

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Popper Police actively patrol and surround player
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must avoid police; arrest triggers transition to Detective Harris interrogation scene

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Detective Frank Harris appears; lecture or bail demand UI/visual display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must pay bail (nickels) or accept lecture; determines progression

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can negotiate trades, access restricted locations, pay bail freely

environmental_storytelling.example: Malt shop restricted to players with sufficient candy; signals progression and player achievement through visual gatekeeping
Police-frequented public spaces vs. civilian entertainment zones (Slash Club, apartment buildings); visual separation of authority vs. freedom spaces
Nickel/coin display and bail system; visually communicates financial consequences and player economic state through UI and location-based transactions
Detective Frank Harris appearance post-arrest; NPC visual presence signals narrative consequence and punishment state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Visible in world; will surround player if player breaks law
Apprehend player; trigger transition to Detective Harris encounter
Delivers lecture or demands bail payment; visual authority figure in interrogation context
Available at Slash Club for interaction/negotiation
Awaits player at apartment; reaching her triggers progression; failing to reach her results in player reset
Must evade Popper Police; risk of surrounding and arrest

notes: Cool World SNES uses a unique adventure-game structure compared to other platform versions, featuring a law/authority dynamic with the Popper Police as primary antagonistic faction. The game communicates world-state through location accessibility, NPC interactions (especially Detective Harris and Holli Would), and the police pursuit/arrest mechanic. The cartoon aesthetic from the source film carries through in visual faction presentation. Economic resources (nickels) serve as both progression gate and consequence mechanism (bail payment). The game's core tension is communicating 'legal consequence' as a persistent environmental threat.
